<div class="py-4">
  <h2 id="all-intel" class="mb-4">All INTEL</h2>

  <div class="row g-4">
    <div class="col-12">
      <div class="card p-4">
        <h3 id="intel-7" class="h5"><a href="/intel/intel-7-risk-profile-inversion/">INTEL-7: A New Risk Has Entered the Top 5</a></h3>
        <p class="text-muted mb-2"><em>March 26, 2026</em> | <code>TREND</code></p>
        <p class="mb-0">Ransomware encryption dropped 38% in one year. Credential theft at 23% is now double encryption. Undetected long-term access has become a top-5 risk most organizations haven't added to their register.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="col-12">
      <div class="card p-4">
        <h3 id="intel-6" class="h5"><a href="/intel/intel-6-self-aware-malware/">INTEL-6: The Malware That Does Math to Prove You're Human</a></h3>
        <p class="text-muted mb-2"><em>March 25, 2026</em> | <code>TECHNIQUE</code></p>
        <p class="mb-0">LummaC2 calculates Euclidean distance and angles of mouse cursor paths using trigonometry to detect sandboxes. Sandbox evasion surged into the top 5 most prevalent techniques for the first time.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="col-12">
      <div class="card p-4">
        <h3 id="intel-5" class="h5"><a href="/intel/intel-5-microsoft-identity-trust-exploitation/">INTEL-5: Your Microsoft Login Page Is the Phishing Page</a></h3>
        <p class="text-muted mb-2"><em>March 11, 2026</em> | <code>THREAT ALERT</code></p>
        <p class="mb-0">Nation-state adversaries from Russia and Iran are weaponizing Microsoft's own authentication infrastructure — Entra ID, OAuth 2.0, and device code flows — to gain persistent access that traditional security controls cannot detect.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="col-12">
      <div class="card p-4">
        <h3 id="intel-4" class="h5"><a href="/intel/intel-4-muddled-libra-cloud-api-abuse/">INTEL-4: Your Cloud APIs Are the Attack Infrastructure</a></h3>
        <p class="text-muted mb-2"><em>March 4, 2026</em> | <code>THREAT ALERT</code></p>
        <p class="mb-0">Muddled Libra doesn't bring malware. They call your help desk, reset a password, and use Microsoft Graph API to own your cloud from the inside. Unit 42's 2026 report flags them across aerospace, finance, tech, and telecom.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="col-12">
      <div class="card p-4">
        <h3 id="intel-3" class="h5"><a href="/intel/intel-3-llm-infrastructure-targeting/">INTEL-3: 91,000 Sessions — Threat Actors Are Mapping Your AI Infrastructure</a></h3>
        <p class="text-muted mb-2"><em>February 11, 2026</em> | <code>TREND</code></p>
        <p class="mb-0">GreyNoise documented 91,000+ reconnaissance sessions against LLM deployments in January 2026. If your AI endpoints are reachable without authentication, assume they've been mapped.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="col-12">
      <div class="card p-4">
        <h3 id="intel-2" class="h5"><a href="/intel/intel-2-human-in-the-loop/">INTEL-2: The Human-in-the-Loop Imperative for AI Security</a></h3>
        <p class="text-muted mb-2"><em>February 4, 2026</em> | <code>TREND</code></p>
        <p class="mb-0">NIST built its detection framework around one assumption: AI detects, humans validate. Fully automated security is a design flaw, not a feature.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="col-12">
      <div class="card p-4">
        <h3 id="intel-1" class="h5"><a href="/intel/intel-1-law-firms-ransomware/">INTEL-1: Law Firms Are Now Premium Ransomware Targets</a></h3>
        <p class="text-muted mb-2"><em>January 29, 2026</em> | <code>SECTOR ALERT</code></p>
        <p class="mb-0">Law firms paid ransoms at a 41% rate in 2025 — about 14% more often than the cross-sector average. Average demand: $1.2M.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
